summary: All 5 SAST findings (path injection via user-controlled filename) persisted across all 3 attempts because the code never implemented any sanitization or allowlist for the filename. Despite 2 security fixer attempts, the code continued to use user-supplied filenames directly in file operations (save, exists, send_file, remove) without restricting path traversal characters.
suggested_actions: Implement strict filename validation: reject filenames containing '..' or '/' or null bytes.
Use an allowlist of allowed characters (e.g., alphanumeric, dash, underscore) or generate a safe filename server-side.
Consider using os.path.basename() to strip directory components, but still insufficient alone; combine with allowlist.
Ensure the fix is applied to all 5 vulnerable lines (upload, download, delete).
Add unit tests that verify path traversal attempts are rejected.
